text: Risk management is an important process in any business or project. It begins with identifying risks, which means looking for potential problems that could cause delays or extra costs. After identifying these risks, teams create mitigation strategies designed to reduce their likelihood or impact. For example, they might train staff or improve safety measures. However, not all risks can be avoided, so it is essential to have contingency plans. These are backup actions ready to be used if a risk actually occurs. Finally, effective risk management requires constant monitoring. By regularly checking for new risks and reviewing existing ones, a team can stay prepared and react quickly when problems arise.
